name: Giant Sniper Mean-Reversion
description: Same shape as Strategy 11 — top-5 mega-caps + RSI(2) dip entry — but with two structural differences. (1) Universe is the top 5 by market cap *across all sectors*, not tech-only, so the basket can include non-tech leaders. (2) Has an explicit bear-market shield: when QQQ falls below its 200-day SMA, the strategy liquidates everything regardless of individual-name signals. Exits are per-name on RSI(2) > 70 instead of price highs.
overfit_notes: Two textbook indicators (RSI(2) and SMA(200)) with standard thresholds (<20 / >70 / 200d). Dynamic top-5 universe removes name-selection bias. The bear-market shield is the only added complexity and uses the canonical 200d cutoff. A clean, replicable design.

rules.label: Universe
rules.text: Top 5 US stocks by market cap (all sectors, dynamic)

rules.label: Trend shield
rules.text: QQQ > SMA(200) required to hold anything

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: Bull regime + per-name RSI(2) < 20 → buy at equal weight

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Per-name RSI(2) > 70; OR QQQ < SMA(200) → liquidate all

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Dynamic — typically AAPL/MSFT/NVDA/GOOG/AMZN etc.

name: Mega-Cap Value Averaging
description: Universe-driven dip-buy on the five largest-cap U.S. stocks (selected from the top 100 by dollar volume, then ranked by market cap each universe refresh). Whenever a name pulls back more than 5% from its 20-day high it gets a 20% portfolio allocation, and the position is liquidated the moment price prints a new 20-day high. The construction sidesteps single-stock bets by spreading 100% nominal exposure across five mega-caps, and only sells into strength — never on weakness — so a position can sit in drawdown indefinitely until a fresh high releases it.
overfit_notes: Two tuned numbers (5% dip threshold, 20-day high lookback) and a hindsight-driven universe — 'top 5 by market cap' over 2014–2025 maps directly onto the mega-cap tech complex (AAPL/MSFT/AMZN/GOOGL/META/NVDA), which is the regime the backtest favors. The rule itself (buy weakness in an uptrend, exit on new highs) is a standard value-averaging template; no exotic indicators or per-asset tuning.

rules.label: Universe
rules.text: Top 100 stocks by dollar volume → top 5 by market cap (daily refresh)

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: Price < 20-day high × 0.95 → buy 20% allocation

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Price ≥ 20-day high → liquidate

rules.label: Sizing
rules.text: Fixed 20% per name (5 names → 100% gross when fully loaded)

rules.label: Resolution
rules.text: Daily bars

name: 5 most mkt cap + IBS regime mix
description: Two-regime rotation on the top 5 US stocks by market cap. **Uptrend** (QQQ > 200d SMA): hold all 5 mega-caps equal-weight (20% each). **Downtrend** (QQQ ≤ 200d SMA): rotate into only those names whose IBS < 0.2 (close near day's low — buying weakness in mega-caps only when the broader market is bearish). Position weights adjust daily based on how many names meet the bear-mode filter.
overfit_notes: Dynamic universe removes name-selection bias. SMA(200) and IBS<0.2 are both canonical thresholds. The 5% rebalance drift tolerance is a minor magic number but isn't outcome-shaping. One of the cleanest hybrid designs — mirrors Strategies.md Strategy-9 (active production version) at the same rating.

rules.label: Universe
rules.text: Top 5 US stocks by market cap (all sectors, dynamic)

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ > SMA(200) → 'trend' mode; else → 'MR' mode

rules.label: Allocation (trend)
rules.text: Equal weight all 5 names (20% each); rebalance only when drift > 5%

rules.label: Allocation (MR)
rules.text: Equal weight names with IBS < 0.2 (variable subset, 0-5 names)

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Per-name: liquidated when no longer in target set (universe change or filter exit)

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Dynamic — typically AAPL/MSFT/NVDA/GOOG/AMZN etc.; QQQ for signal

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily at 10:30 ET

name: Nasdaq-100 Breadth Rotation
description: Uses participation across the 10 largest-cap U.S. stocks (drawn from the top 100 by dollar volume) as a breadth regime gate for TQQQ. Each constituent runs a 50-day EMA, and the strategy measures the fraction trading above its EMA: above 60% it goes 100% long TQQQ, below 40% it liquidates, and in the 40–60% no-man's-land it holds whatever it had. The mega-cap basket acts as a proxy for Nasdaq leadership health — when participation is broad the engine ramps full 3× exposure, and when it deteriorates it steps fully aside.
overfit_notes: Three tuned thresholds (50-day EMA period, 60% bull cutoff, 40% bear cutoff) and a regime-defining universe choice. The 60/40 asymmetric hysteresis is a reasonable design choice (prevents whipsaws around 50%) but the specific levels are unverified. The 'top 10 by market cap' basket suffers the same hindsight asset-selection bias as #14 — over 2014–2025 it locks in the mega-cap tech leadership that the strategy then leverages 3× into.

rules.label: Universe
rules.text: Top 100 stocks by dollar volume → top 10 by market cap (signal basket)

rules.label: Indicator
rules.text: 50-day EMA per constituent; breadth = fraction trading above EMA

rules.label: Bull entry
rules.text: Breadth > 60% → 100% TQQQ

rules.label: Bear exit
rules.text: Breadth < 40% → liquidate TQQQ

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: top-10 mega-cap basket. Execution: TQQQ

rules.label: Resolution
rules.text: Daily bars

name: Mega-Cap Dispersion Regime
description: A breadth-based trend follower that adds a cohesion filter to the standard D200 regime: it only holds TQQQ when both QQQ is in an uptrend and the five largest US stocks are moving together, as measured by the standard deviation of their 20-day returns being below a threshold. High dispersion among mega-caps signals stress and triggers a shift to cash.
overfit_notes: Three parameters: D200 midpoint regime, the top-5 market-cap universe, and a 5% cross-sectional standard deviation threshold on 20-day returns. The 5% dispersion cutoff is a specific tuned value.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ price > midpoint of 200-day high/low range

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: Std dev of 20-day returns across top-5 mega-caps < 5%

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: In trend AND dispersion < 5% → 100% TQQQ

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Trend breaks OR dispersion ≥ 5% → 100% cash

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ + top-5 mega-cap dispersion. Execution: TQQQ

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open

name: UpDnVol+52w+Top3
description: A volume-breadth rotator that measures whether buying pressure (up-day volume) exceeds selling pressure (down-day volume) over 20 days, combined with a 52-week proximity filter (price within 7% of the one-year high). Both signals assess market health from different angles — participation from the volume side and strength from the drawdown side — alongside the 200-bar median regime gate.
overfit_notes: Up-volume vs down-volume over 20 bars; drawdown threshold of 93% of 252-day high; median-of-200 regime. The 93% threshold and 252-day window are specific tuned parameters. Rated 4/10.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ price > median of last 200 closes

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: 20-day up-day volume > 20-day down-day volume; price > 93% of 252-day rolling high

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: Score 3 → 100% TQQQ; score 2 → 50% TQQQ + 50% Top 3; score 1 → 100% Top 3; score 0 → 50% Top 3 + 50% cash

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / large-cap Top 3

rules.label: Universe
rules.text: Top 100 by dollar volume → Top 3 by market cap

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open

name: Mom20+52w+Top3
description: Combines a 20-day momentum signal with a 52-week high proximity filter and a 200-bar median regime gate. Being near the 52-week high is a well-known indicator of trend strength. The short-term momentum adds a recency confirmation. Together they construct a three-signal score that governs allocation between the leveraged ETF, large-cap stocks, and cash.
overfit_notes: 20-day momentum, 52-week proximity at 93% of 252-day max, median-of-200. The 93% threshold for 52w proximity is tuned. Rated 4/10.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ price > median of last 200 closes

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: 20-day price momentum positive; price > 93% of 252-day rolling high

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: Score 3 → 100% TQQQ; score 2 → 50% TQQQ + 50% Top 3; score 1 → 100% Top 3; score 0 → 50% Top 3 + 50% cash

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / large-cap Top 3

rules.label: Universe
rules.text: Top 100 by dollar volume → Top 3 by market cap

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open

name: M252_NEAR60_VOLCONT+Top3
description: A trend-following rotator combining a full-year momentum check with near-60-day-high strength confirmation and a volatility contraction condition. The yearly lookback selects for macro uptrends, while the shorter-term filters ensure the current price action is both strong and calm before adding risk. Allocations are spread across TQQQ, the top three large-cap stocks, and cash on the standard five-level ladder.
overfit_notes: Momentum compares close[-1] vs close[0] over 252 bars; near-60 threshold is 95%; volatility contraction compares 10-bar and 60-bar stddev windows; median trend gate uses the 101st value of 200 closes. The combination of a long-term momentum lookback with short-term vol filters is a specific structural choice.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ price > median close of last 200 days (f0)

rules.label: Entry (trend)
rules.text: f1: QQQ close[-1] > close[0] (~252-day momentum, current vs. year-ago)

rules.label: Entry (trend)
rules.text: f2: QQQ price ≥ 95% of 60-day high

rules.label: Entry (trend)
rules.text: f3: 10-bar realized vol < 60-bar realized vol (volatility contraction)

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / top-3 large-cap stocks by market cap

rules.label: Universe
rules.text: Top 100 by dollar volume → top 3 by market cap (fine selection), refreshed daily

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open; only trades when score n changes

name: OBV20_CCI20
description: Pairs monthly OBV momentum with CCI on QQQ. Requires OBV rising over 20 days plus CCI > 0 for leveraged Nasdaq, and rotates to T-bills on either OBV weakness or deeply oversold CCI.
overfit_notes: Three tuned levers: OBV(20)-up, CCI(20)>0, and CCI<-100 with AND/OR logic

rules.label: Entry (trend)
rules.text: OBV(20)-up AND CCI(20) > 0: 100% TQQQ

rules.label: Entry (neutral)
rules.text: Mixed: equal-weight top-5 large-caps

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: OBV(20)-down OR CCI < -100: 100% cash

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / top-5 fundamental large-caps

rules.label: Universe
rules.text: Top 100 by dollar volume, then top 5 by market cap

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open (only on regime change)

name: OBV20_ADX14
description: Combines monthly OBV momentum with ADX directional bias on QQQ. Requires OBV rising plus +DI > -DI for leveraged Nasdaq, and exits to T-bills on either OBV weakness or a confirmed bearish ADX regime.
overfit_notes: Four tuned components: OBV(20)-up, +DI vs -DI direction, ADX(14)>25 bearish floor, and AND/OR combination

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: Bearish trigger requires ADX(14) > 25 AND -DI > +DI

rules.label: Entry (trend)
rules.text: OBV(20)-up AND +DI > -DI: 100% TQQQ

rules.label: Entry (neutral)
rules.text: Mixed: equal-weight top-5 large-caps

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: OBV(20)-down OR (ADX > 25 AND -DI > +DI): 100% cash

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / top-5 fundamental large-caps

rules.label: Universe
rules.text: Top 100 by dollar volume, then top 5 by market cap

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open (only on regime change)

name: Tech Dip Buy
description: Buys the top 5 US technology stocks by market cap when they pull back hard during an uptrend, then holds until they recover to new highs or the loss gets too large. Universe rotates automatically as market caps shift. Equal-weight across held positions up to 20% per slot.
overfit_notes: Textbook RSI(2)/SMA(50) entry with a clean 15% hard stop; dynamic universe removes hindsight symbol selection. Only mild concern is the small trade count (~80 trades over 11 years).

rules.label: Universe
rules.text: Top 5 US tech stocks by market cap (Morningstar Technology sector)

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: RSI(2) < 30 AND price > SMA(50): up to 20% per name

rules.label: Stop loss
rules.text: Price ≤ avg cost × 0.85: liquidate

rules.label: Take profit
rules.text: Price ≥ 252-day high: liquidate

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Weekly (Mondays), 30 min after market open

name: Top-5 MarketCap IBS Regime
description: Dynamically selects the top 5 US equities by market capitalization as the universe. In a bull regime (QQQ above its 200-day SMA), holds an equal-weight basket of all 5. In a bear regime, applies an IBS < 0.2 filter and holds only the stocks closing near their day's low (capitulation dips); exits the rest.
overfit_notes: Two parameters: SMA(200) regime filter and IBS threshold 0.2. Both are standard values. The top-5 market cap selection is deterministic. Low overfit.

rules.label: Universe
rules.text: Top 5 US stocks by market cap, daily resolution

rules.label: Bull regime
rules.text: QQQ > SMA(200) → equal-weight all 5 positions

rules.label: Bear regime
rules.text: QQQ ≤ SMA(200) → equal-weight only stocks with IBS < 0.2

rules.label: IBS
rules.text: (close−low) / (high−low)

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 45 min after market open
